beats.activity: Wake up, shower, and have coffee at home.
beats.reasoning: Monday morning wake-up routine. Daniel starts the day at full energy with a clear head before the work week.

beats.activity: Drive to Redmond workplace, avoiding Light Rail disruption.
beats.reasoning: Light Rail closure 9am–3pm between Westlake and U-District means driving is the best option. Daniel leaves early enough to arrive by 8:35am, well before the disruption window.

beats.activity: Work on bookstore operations and inventory management.
beats.reasoning: Daniel works at his bookstore in Redmond. Morning focus on operational tasks before the USA vs Wales match at 11am.

beats.activity: Watch USA vs Wales FIFA World Cup match at a local bar downtown.
beats.location_name: Matt's in the Market (Pike Place Market)
beats.reasoning: Daniel attends the 11am match as scheduled. While skeptical of crowds, he makes an exception for World Cup football and enjoys the social atmosphere at a smaller, quieter bar.

beats.activity: Lunch at Pike Place Market after the match.
beats.reasoning: Daniel grabs lunch nearby while downtown. The sunny 74F weather makes the market stalls appealing, and it's convenient after the match.
Returns home from downtown, avoiding the Light Rail closure window. Across-town drive with buffer time.

beats.activity: Urban gardening on the back patio and tending to raised beds.
beats.reasoning: Perfect weather (sunny, 74F) for outdoor gardening. Daniel takes advantage of the beautiful Monday afternoon to maintain his garden beds and relax.

beats.activity: Walk to local neighborhood errand: post office to mail handwritten letters.
beats.reasoning: Daniel maintains his habit of writing and mailing handwritten letters. A short walk in the pleasant afternoon weather to the local post office fits his organized, personal style.

beats.activity: Walk back home from post office.
beats.reasoning: Short walk back home in the pleasant evening light. Neighborhood errand completed.

beats.activity: Video gaming and dinner at home.
beats.reasoning: Daniel unwinds with video games and prepares a simple dinner. A quiet, organized evening at home aligns with his preference for close, low-key time over crowds.

beats.activity: Read, write in journal, and prepare for bed.
beats.reasoning: Evening wind-down routine. Daniel reads, reflects on the day by writing, and prepares for sleep. This sets up a natural transition into the final sleep beat.
Full night of sleep spanning past midnight, ending early morning. Daniel gets a solid 7+ hours before the next day begins.

diary: Monday started quiet—coffee at home, the usual routine before heading to Redmond. But I ditched the morning to catch the USA-Wales match at Matt's in the Market. The bar was packed, energy electric, and honestly? I needed that. Grabbed lunch at Pike Place afterward, wandered through the crowds, felt alive. Got back to Roosevelt and spent a couple hours tending the raised beds on the patio. The tomatoes are coming in nice. Later I walked over to the post office to mail some letters I'd been putting off—something about handwriting feels important, you know? Spent the evening gaming and reading, nothing special, but it felt earned. Good day. Settled.
thought_process: I woke up with this restless energy and the World Cup match felt like permission to break routine. Work could wait—I managed the morning tasks remotely anyway. Watching the game with strangers cheering was exactly what I needed after months of quiet bookstore life. The light rail closure didn't bother me; drove instead, no stress. Pike Place grounded me afterward. Back home, the garden felt meditative, and mailing those handwritten letters scratched an itch I didn't know I had. By evening I was genuinely tired, content. The day had rhythm: social energy, then solitude, then reflection. My mood shifted from restless to settled.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
